chapters.title: Prepared for: 22-Apr-1975 Mysuru and 30-May-2026 Athlone
chapters.content_preview: Prepared for: 22-Apr-1975 Mysuru and 30-May-2026 Athlone  Version 1.0 • Generated 30 May 2026  Reader’s orientation  This handbook separates three things that are often mixed up: (1) the time division called Do-Ghati Muhurta, (2) the fixed muhurta-lord Nakshatra mapping used by P

chapters.title: Reader’s orientation
chapters.content_preview: Reader’s orientation  This handbook separates three things that are often mixed up: (1) the time division called Do-Ghati Muhurta, (2) the fixed muhurta-lord Nakshatra mapping used by Panchang systems, and (3) the practical purpose/avoidance guidance derived from muhurta and Naks

chapters.title: Sunset to next sunrise = 30 ghatis

chapters.title: Fixed 48 minutes?
chapters.content_preview: Fixed 48 minutes?  Only approximately true near equal day/night  The actual slot length changes by date and location.  Important correction

chapters.title: Only approximately true near equal day/night
chapters.content_preview: Only approximately true near equal day/night  The actual slot length changes by date and location.  Important correction  Do not assume every muhurta is exactly 48 minutes. For example, on 30-May-2026 in Athlone, the day slots are about 66m 26s each and the night slots are only a

chapters.title: Important correction
chapters.content_preview: Important correction  Do not assume every muhurta is exactly 48 minutes. For example, on 30-May-2026 in Athlone, the day slots are about 66m 26s each and the night slots are only about 29m 30s each because summer daylight is long at that latitude.  2. Source Basis and Authenticit

chapters.title: What it contributes
chapters.content_preview: What it contributes  Used for  Vedic/Brahmana background  Texts such as the Taittiriya Brahmana and Satapatha Brahmana preserve muhūrta as an ancient time measure and ritual time concept. This supports the time-unit foundation, not every later election rule. [S4]

chapters.title: Used for
chapters.content_preview: Used for  Vedic/Brahmana background  Texts such as the Taittiriya Brahmana and Satapatha Brahmana preserve muhūrta as an ancient time measure and ritual time concept. This supports the time-unit foundation, not every later election rule. [S4]  Primary-text background

chapters.title: Vedic/Brahmana background
chapters.content_preview: Vedic/Brahmana background  Texts such as the Taittiriya Brahmana and Satapatha Brahmana preserve muhūrta as an ancient time measure and ritual time concept. This supports the time-unit foundation, not every later election rule. [S4]  Primary-text background  Panchang practice

chapters.title: Primary-text background
chapters.content_preview: Primary-text background  Panchang practice  Modern Panchang references describe the day as sunrise-to-sunrise, split day/night into ghatis, and list the 30 Do-Ghati Muhurtas with auspicious/inauspicious status. [S1]  Calculation + names/status

chapters.title: Panchang practice
chapters.content_preview: Panchang practice  Modern Panchang references describe the day as sunrise-to-sunrise, split day/night into ghatis, and list the 30 Do-Ghati Muhurtas with auspicious/inauspicious status. [S1]  Calculation + names/status  Nakshatra mapping

chapters.title: Calculation + names/status
chapters.content_preview: Calculation + names/status  Nakshatra mapping  The fixed mapping used here follows the table where 15 day muhurtas and 15 night muhurtas are allotted to Nakshatra lords. [S2]  Mapping table

chapters.title: Nakshatra mapping
chapters.content_preview: Nakshatra mapping  The fixed mapping used here follows the table where 15 day muhurtas and 15 night muhurtas are allotted to Nakshatra lords. [S2]  Mapping table  Purpose interpretation

chapters.title: Mapping table
chapters.content_preview: Mapping table  Purpose interpretation  B. V. Raman’s Muhurtha summarises Nakshatra natures from Brihat Samhita tradition: fixed, soft, light, sharp, movable, dreadful and mixed; these categories are used to infer suitable/unsuitable work types. [S3]  Purpose guide

chapters.title: Purpose interpretation
chapters.content_preview: Purpose interpretation  B. V. Raman’s Muhurtha summarises Nakshatra natures from Brihat Samhita tradition: fixed, soft, light, sharp, movable, dreadful and mixed; these categories are used to infer suitable/unsuitable work types. [S3]  Purpose guide  Honesty note: The activity de

chapters.title: Purpose guide
chapters.content_preview: Purpose guide  Honesty note: The activity descriptions in the master table are a practical synthesis, not a claim that one single Vedic verse lists all modern uses. The exact timing method and fixed Nakshatra mapping are source-based; the use guide blends muhurta name, deity symb

chapters.title: Astronomical apparent sunrise/sunset; local timezone applied.
chapters.content_preview: Astronomical apparent sunrise/sunset; local timezone applied.  2  Day muhurta duration = (sunset − sunrise) ÷ 15.  Slots 1–15.

chapters.title: Slots 1–15.
chapters.content_preview: Slots 1–15.  3  Night muhurta duration = (next sunrise − sunset) ÷ 15.  Slots 16–30; past-midnight times belong to the same Panchang day.

chapters.title: Round display times to nearest minute.
chapters.content_preview: Round display times to nearest minute.  Underlying calculations were done to seconds; table times are for easy reference.  Nakshatra mapping vs actual Moon Nakshatra  In Do-Ghati tables, the Nakshatra column is a fixed muhurta-lord mapping: slot 1 corresponds to Ardra, slot 2 to

chapters.title: Nakshatra mapping vs actual Moon Nakshatra
chapters.content_preview: Nakshatra mapping vs actual Moon Nakshatra  In Do-Ghati tables, the Nakshatra column is a fixed muhurta-lord mapping: slot 1 corresponds to Ardra, slot 2 to Ashlesha by day / Purva Bhadrapada by night, and so on. This is different from the Moon’s real-time Nakshatra on that date.

chapters.title: What to do
chapters.content_preview: What to do  1  Purpose fit  Choose a slot whose muhurta, Nakshatra nature and status match the action. Do not use a sharp/dreadful slot for a gentle ceremony unless there is a compelling reason.

chapters.title: Purpose fit
chapters.content_preview: Purpose fit  Choose a slot whose muhurta, Nakshatra nature and status match the action. Do not use a sharp/dreadful slot for a gentle ceremony unless there is a compelling reason.  2  Panchanga shuddhi

chapters.title: Panchanga shuddhi
chapters.content_preview: Panchanga shuddhi  Check Tithi, Vara, actual lunar Nakshatra, Yoga and Karana. A good Do-Ghati slot does not override a badly afflicted full Panchanga.  3  Tara-bala

chapters.title: Sandhya awareness
chapters.content_preview: Sandhya awareness  The beginnings and endings of day/night are traditionally reserved for worship, transition and purification; avoid rushing worldly launches there unless required.  7  When forced

chapters.title: When forced
chapters.content_preview: When forced  Use the principle of more good than harm: select the least problematic slot, strengthen the intent with prayer/remedies and avoid known tyajya/visha portions.  Best-Fit Examples  Task

chapters.title: Two ghatis; one muhūrta slot in this system.
chapters.content_preview: Two ghatis; one muhūrta slot in this system.  Muhurta  A selected or measured time segment; in this handbook, one of the 30 Do-Ghati divisions.  Nakshatra

chapters.title: Final caution
chapters.content_preview: Final caution  This handbook is deliberately practical, but advanced Muhurta is layered. Use the Do-Ghati slot as one filter. For life events such as marriage, griha pravesha, surgery, major business launch or consecration, a competent Jyotisha/Panchanga practitioner should verif
